title: Projected Volume: ConfigMap and Secret Together
description:
## Projected Volume: Combining ConfigMap and Secret

A **projected volume** merges multiple volume sources — ConfigMaps, Secrets, ServiceAccount tokens, and `downwardAPI` — into a **single directory** inside the container. This avoids cluttering the container with multiple separate mounts.

**Your task:**

Create a Pod named `projected-pod` that uses a **single projected volume** mounted at `/config` combining:
1. A ConfigMap named `app-settings` with key `mode=debug`
2. A Secret named `app-token` with key `token=s3cr3t`

Both files should appear side-by-side at `/config/mode` and `/config/token` inside the container.

```bash
# Verify both files are present:
kubectl exec projected-pod -- ls /config
kubectl exec projected-pod -- cat /config/mode
kubectl exec projected-pod -- cat /config/token
```

hints.title: Create the ConfigMap and Secret first
hints.body: Create both source objects before the pod that mounts them.
hints.command: kubectl create configmap app-settings --from-literal=mode=debug && kubectl create secret generic app-token --from-literal=token=s3cr3t

hints.title: Projected volume manifest
hints.body: Use volumes[].projected.sources[] to list multiple sources. Each source has a configMap or secret key with the source name.
hints.command:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: projected-pod
spec:
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: busybox:1.36
    command: ["sleep", "3600"]
    volumeMounts:
    - name: combined-config
      mountPath: /config
  volumes:
  - name: combined-config
    projected:
      sources:
      - configMap:
          name: app-settings
      - secret:
          name: app-token
EOF
